channel.title: Tech Blog
channel.description: Cutting-edge insights into technology, AI, and development

item.title: RAG in production? Skip the toy demos
item.description: RAG in production fails when tutorials ignore metadata filters, IVFFlat indexing, and reranking — we cut latency 88% and costs 87% with Postgres + pgvector, FastAPI, and vLLM on T4 GPUs.

item.title: 2026 remote salary parity for LATAM devs
item.description: Generate your 2026 remote counter in 100 lines — parity engine, Mercer index, and the negotiation script top engineers use. Avoid the $75k anchor trap.

item.title: Senior devs quit for more than pay
item.description: Cut on-call pages 90%: data from 2026 shows senior devs flee big tech for autonomy, observability, and blameless culture — not just salary.

item.title: Pick real-time tech: WebSockets, SSE, or polling?
item.description: A 2026 Stack Overflow survey found that 37% of teams that adopted WebSockets later had to roll back at least one major feature because they underestimated…

item.title: Skip CPU cores for DB pools
item.description: Cut database connection errors 80% by ignoring CPU cores—use max_connections and actual demand instead. Benchmarks, pitfalls, and the one setting every team configures wrong.

item.title: Show benchmarks, not GitHub stars
item.description: In my experience, this advice is only half right — and for 70% of African developers I’ve mentored, it’s actively harmful.

item.title: Which AI Skills Actually Raise Your Salary in 2026
item.description: In 2026, only 2 AI skills move your salary past $180k. RAG at scale and fine-tuning small models are the only levers with proven ROI. See the data.

item.title: Doctors coding apps at 3am: the stack they trust
item.description: The teams I’ve worked with that started as side projects in clinics, law firms, and labs now run systems handling thousands of requests per second with…

item.title: AI salary killers: PyTorch vs TensorFlow 2026
item.description: PyTorch inductor vs TensorFlow tf.function 2026 benchmark: who actually gets paid more? Real numbers on training speed, CPU latency, and salary premiums.

item.title: Close the geography pay gap in 5 steps
item.description: Close the remote salary gap in 30 minutes using Wise fees, Numbeo data, and a 3-tab spreadsheet — real 2026 results from engineers in Colombia, Mexico, and Brazil

item.title: Compare WebSockets, SSE, long polling costs
item.description: Cut push-tech latency and cost: WebSocket vs SSE vs long polling benchmarks for 2026 — pick the right tool in 15 minutes

item.title: Pool settings that waste your database money
item.description: Cut API p99 latency 60% and cloud bill 8-18% by fixing connection pool settings — peak concurrent queries, idle timeout 600000 ms, and PostgreSQL 16 limits

item.title: Hidden costs of production RAG pipelines
item.description: Production RAG pipelines fail on latency, cost, and memory unless you compress first, tier second, and cache last. Here’s the hidden tax most tutorials ignore — with real numbers from a Jakarta startup’s 50k-doc chatbot.

item.title: Senior devs flee big tech for small stakes
item.description: When the only way to get a 1% performance gain is to file three Jira tickets, wait for code review from someone who hasn’t touched the code in 18 months…

item.title: Ship messy systems: Africa devs win remote jobs
item.description: Prove you can solve real problems: build a portfolio with benchmarks, trade-offs, and collaboration artifacts — not bios. 78% of remote managers prioritize practical contributions over polished profiles.

item.title: 2026 freelance dev rates, unmapped
item.description: 2026 freelance developer rates decoded: exact bands, real deliverables, and the 1.5× risk factor most devs miss. Stop leaving money on the table.

item.title: WebSockets vs SSE vs long-polling: which fits?
item.description: SSE costs 45% less than WebSockets and 80% less than long polling at 1000 users/day — benchmark results from Node 20 LTS and Redis 7.2.

item.title: 5 rules for low-cost remote pay
item.description: Convert any remote offer to real take-home with Python 3.11 — FX rates, taxes, time-zone penalties and RSU discounts in 2026

item.title: AI skills that pay: backend vs prompt skills in 2026
item.description: Backend AI skills pay 8-15% more than prompt skills in 2026 — benchmarked against Redis 7.2 and LangChain 0.1.12. See latency, cost, and salary differences.

item.title: Show salary, not screenshots: Africa dev portfolios
item.description: Portfolio flops when it’s just screenshots — real remote jobs in 2026 demand production artifacts: load tests, alerts, runbooks, cost reports. Learn the exact artifacts that got African engineers $70k+ remote offers.
